Econometric Battery — Table 15 Metrics

Methodology & Data Sources

Chart Lines

Prediction Market — Kalshi or Polymarket last-trade daily price for each strike bucket.
Black-Scholes — theoretical probability from the Black-Scholes digital-call formula using 30-day implied volatility and the FRED DGS3MO risk-free rate.
Options / BL — Breeden-Litzenberger implied probability from CBOE end-of-day option prices (SPX Dec 2023 only).
Spot Price — underlying asset spot (right axis), where available.

Breeden-Litzenberger (Options) Method — SPX Dec 2023

The gold Options (BL) line extracts the market's risk-neutral probability distribution directly from observed SPX option prices, without assuming a log-normal return distribution. The method is due to Breeden & Litzenberger (1978).

Core theorem. For a European call option C(K) on an underlying with current price S, risk-free rate r, and time to expiry T, the risk-neutral PDF q(K) at all possible terminal prices K satisfies:

q(K) = erT · ∂²C / ∂K²

In words: the second derivative of the call price with respect to the strike equals (up to discounting) the risk-neutral probability density at that strike level.

Why this is a butterfly spread. A long butterfly spread is constructed by buying one call at strike K−ΔK, selling two calls at K, and buying one call at K+ΔK. Its payoff is exactly 1 if the underlying lands near K and 0 otherwise. In the limit as ΔK → 0, the no-arbitrage price of this butterfly equals e−rT ⋅ q(K) ⋅ ΔK, which is precisely the BL formula above. So the second derivative is effectively the cost of an infinitely tight butterfly at strike K — a direct market price for the probability of landing there.

Implementation steps.

  1. Source data. CBOE end-of-day SPX options files (one per calendar month, 2023). For each trading day we extract all strikes for the Dec 29, 2023 expiry and their implied volatilities (C_IV column, already computed by CBOE).
  2. IV spline. We fit a cubic spline through the (strike, IV) pairs on that day, restricted to the moneyness range 0.5S – 2.0S. This smooths gaps and allows evaluation at any strike continuously.
  3. Synthetic call prices. We evaluate the spline on a fine grid of 2,000 evenly-spaced strikes Ki, then compute C(Ki) using the standard Black-Scholes formula with each splined IV.
  4. Second numerical derivative. The PDF is:

    q(Ki) = erT ⋅ [C(K+ΔK) − 2C(K) + C(K−ΔK)] / ΔK²

    Negative values (arbitrage violations from spline noise) are clipped to zero; the PDF is then normalized so it integrates to 1 over the strike grid.
  5. Bucket probabilities. For each Kalshi strike bucket [Klo, Khi] we numerically integrate the PDF using the trapezoid rule:

    P(Klo ≤ ST ≤ Khi) = ∫KloKhi q(K) dK

    This gives the options market's implied probability that SPX closes within the bucket on Dec 29, 2023 — equivalent to the fair value of the corresponding Kalshi binary contract under risk-neutral pricing.
  6. Risk-free rate. Daily 3-month Treasury yield (FRED DGS3MO) is used as r for both the synthetic call prices and the erT discount factor.

Limitation. BL requires a deep, liquid options market. SPX options are among the most liquid in the world, so the method is well-suited here. It is not applied to NASDAQ, BTC, Gold, or Eggs because exchange-based EOD option chains for those Kalshi-equivalent strikes are not available or sufficiently liquid for the full contract period.

Black-Scholes Benchmark (all other assets)

For non-SPX assets, the Black-Scholes probability is computed from the digital-call formula: P(ST ≥ K) = N(d2), where d2 = [ln(S/K) + (r − ½σ²)T] / (σ√T). For a range bucket [Klo, Khi] the probability is N(d2,lo) − N(d2,hi). Volatility σ is the 30-day realized historical volatility updated daily.

Asset Reference

AssetPlatformBenchmarkSource
SPXKalshiBlack-Scholes (VIX-based) + BL Options (2023)Kalshi API + CBOE EOD / Yahoo Finance
NASDAQKalshiBlack-Scholes (NDX IV)Kalshi API + Yahoo Finance
GoldPolymarketBlack-Scholes (GLD IV)Polymarket API + Yahoo Finance
BTCPolymarketBlack-ScholesPolymarket API + Yahoo Finance
ETHPolymarketBlack-ScholesPolymarket API + Yahoo Finance
EggsPolymarketBlack-ScholesPolymarket API + USDA
Fed RatesKalshi + PolymarketCME FedWatch implied probabilityKalshi API / Polymarket API + CME

Fed Rates Note

For Fed Rates the "Black-Scholes" line is the CME FedWatch implied probability (not a BS formula). The PM line is from Kalshi or Polymarket depending on the meeting. Dates shown are calendar days leading up to the actual FOMC decision date.
